properties.displayName: [WEEKLY] Linux LPE chain — anomalous algif_aead/esp4/esp6/rxrpc kernel-module load followed by same-user root transition
properties.description:
Detects the behavioural pattern shared by CVE-2026-31431 'Copy Fail' (Theori, April 30; CISA KEV May 1; active exploitation by May 4) and its 'Dirty Frag' successor (CVE-2026-43284 esp4/esp6, CVE-2026-43500 rxrpc; PoC May 7-8 per Microsoft Defender, Unit 42 and Cyber Security News): an unprivileged Linux user triggers an autoload or explicit modprobe/insmod of an otherwise-rare networking/crypto kernel module (algif_aead, esp4, esp6, rxrpc, xfrm_user, af_alg) and within 10 minutes a process owned by that same user spawns a root-owned child or transitions to euid=0 on the same host. The chain spans the exploit phase (T1068 privilege escalation via kernel-module abuse / T1547.006) and is the convergence point Microsoft, Unit 42 and CISA all called out this fortnight regardless of how the unprivileged shell was obtained (web RCE per Article 6 PAN-OS, container break-out per Article 11, malicious package per Article 2's supply-chain framing, or AI-agent RCE per Article 9). Fires when stage 1 (kmod load) and stage 2 (root transition by the same actor) both occur within the correlation window; will NOT fire on routine boot-time module loads by systemd-modules-load/udevd, on dpkg/apt/yum/snapd/unattended-upgrade installs, on configuration-management agents (ansible/puppet/chef/salt) bringing modules in, or on root sessions where no anomalous kmod load preceded.

Rationale: Every article in scope — Copy Fail PoC, CISA KEV listing, Dirty Frag esp4/esp6/rxrpc disclosure, Microsoft Defender's active-attack post and Unit 42's deep-dive — converges on the same observable: an unprivileged actor causes an unusual kernel networking/crypto module to be brought into the running kernel and then immediately gains root on that host. That two-stage shape is structurally rare in production (legitimate module loads come from boot-time systemd-modules-load or the package manager, never from a user-context parent) and resists IOC rotation because the modules themselves are the vulnerable code paths, not the exploit payload.
properties.query:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CorrelationWindow = 10m;
let _baseline_parents = dynamic(["systemd","systemd-udevd","udevd","init","kmod","systemd-modules-load","dpkg","apt","apt-get","yum","dnf","zypper","snapd","unattended-upgrade","ansible-playbook","puppet","chef-client","salt-minion"]);
let _root_legit_parents = dynamic(["systemd","init","cron","crond","sshd","login","getty","agetty","gdm","gdm3","lightdm","systemd-logind","dbus-daemon","rsyslogd"]);
let _suspect_modules = dynamic(["algif_aead","esp4","esp6","rxrpc","xfrm_user","af_alg"]);
let KmodStage =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where DvcOs has "Linux" or EventProduct in~ ("auoms","sysmon-linux","Defender for Endpoint")
    | where TargetProcessName endswith "/modprobe" or TargetProcessName endswith "/insmod" or TargetProcessName endswith "/kmod"
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (_suspect_modules)
    | where ParentProcessName !in~ (_baseline_parents)
        and not(ParentProcessName endswith "/systemd")
        and not(ParentProcessName endswith "/udevd")
    | where ActorUsername !in~ ("root","") and not(ActorUsername endswith "$")
    | project StageOneTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              KmodUser = ActorUsername,
              KmodCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
              KmodParent = ParentProcessName;
let RootStage =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where TargetUsername =~ "root"
    | where ActorUsername !in~ ("root","")
    | where ParentProcessName !in~ (_root_legit_parents)
    | project StageTwoTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              RootProc = TargetProcessName,
              RootCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine,
              RootParent = ParentProcessName,
              RootParentUser = ActorUsername;
KmodStage
| join kind=inner RootStage on DvcHostname
| where StageTwoTime between (StageOneTime .. StageOneTime + CorrelationWindow)
| where RootParentUser =~ KmodUser
| project StageOneTime, StageTwoTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', StageTwoTime, StageOneTime),
          DvcHostname, KmodUser, KmodParent, KmodCmd,
          RootParent, RootProc, RootCmd
| order by StageOneTime desc
